Into the Abyss.say: There it is! Picking up a reading! Patching up errant data... Aaand done! I've located a meteorite fragment, everyone!
Outstanding! Now it's time to begin the synchronization procedure.
Will do! Finally I get to use the grappling hands on this baby!
Watch this – I'm about to pick up this fragment with the most precise and delicate maneuver you've ever seen...
Got it! Now we, uh... Hook it up to the chamber, right?
Yup. I'll go and get started on that.
Oh, by the way, I need an assistant to help me operate it. Arkhangelsk, can I count on you?
Absolutely. I've got the manual right here in case I'll need it.
Great. Then you and I will head over now and start the data-gathering process. We may be gone for some time, just so you all know.
Captain, keep on piloting the sub. Kiev, keep an eye on our surroundings. And Volga, in the case of an emergency, you're in command.
Understood~ We'll hold the fort while you're gone. Be careful with the equipment over there!
Of course. Hehehe, this is the moment of truth...
Suliko Interior - Synchronization Chamber
The small synchronization chamber was filled with instruments, big screens, and control panels, in stark contrast to the barren, functional interiors of the rest of the vessel.
Kronshtadt lay down in a chair resembling a dental engine, and Arkhangelsk took a seat next to her.
This is almost like a doctor's office.
Doctor's office? Seems more like a hi-tech interrogation room to me.
Been to a lot of interrogation rooms, have you, hmm? That's quite a dubious comparison to draw.
Oh please... If there's anything dubious in this room, it's this damned machine.
True. The manual Soyuz gave us is very sparse on details, too... I'm not sure this'll go well.
Well, too late to back out now. Got any vodka on you?
What? Are you seriously thinking about getting plastered?
We're on duty here. I won't stop you if you must, but I will make note of it in my report.
Please, it was a joke. I just need something to distract me from the thought that this machine might do me in.
Sorry, allow me to play along then. "Why yes, I do. Only the finest for you, Miss Kronshtadt."
"Thanks, Arkhangelsk. I raise a toast to good luck. Bottom's up."
Everything's good on our end, Kronshtadt! You can start the synchronization process now!
Got it! Guess that means it's time I put this... helmet-slash-headset thing on.
Yup. Let me do one final check. Artifact is in place... Computer systems online... Data transfer port set to open...
All right. Initiating in 3... 2... 1... Now!

Holiday Getaway.say: Kronshtadt's mind floated through a pure white void.
Moments ago she lay in an uncomfortable chair inside a dreary room. Now it was as though she'd left her own body and soared through the sky.
The experience felt a lot like downing an entire barrel of vodka then getting carted through a desert of snow and ice.
All of a sudden, she was blasted with countless packets of data. Images coalesced into a pixelated mess and overlapping voices became indiscernible from static noise.
How long was she exposed to that storm of information? Seconds? Hours? There's no telling.
But throughout it all, she clung on to her consciousness, lest the storm engulf her. Soon she could feel her own two feet touch the ground.
Ugghh... Why do I have the mother of all hangovers when I've not had a drop to drink...
Getting drunk is the fun part, and I've skipped straight to the horrible day after part...
What the hell happened? Last thing I remember, I was in the Suliko... Where am I now, and how did I get here?
I can smell the sea and feel sunlight on my skin. What is this, a tropical island?
I can move my body freely, but there's no weight to it. Did my soul get teleported to this place...?
Kronshtadt?! H-how did this happen?!
That's my line! I'm the one who was in the synchronization chair!
Yeah, it makes no sense... The machine started whirring, and the next thing I know, I'm here.
And we're not the only ones. Look! Soobrazitelny, Kiev, and Volga all wound up here too!
Mrmhh... Whuh– Where are we....?
You tell me... This is the strangest turn of events I could imagine.
I've figured it out! This is all a dream! A dream where we're in a holiday resort somewhere!
Kiev, pinch my cheek as hard as you can!
As hard as I can? I can do that.
Ow! Oww! OWWWWW! OW! STOP, STOP, TIME OUT! YOU'LL PUNCH A HOLE THROUGH IT! STOOOOOOP!
Oww... That was excruciating! At least in the process, we figured out this isn't a dream since we can feel pain!
Aww, that sounded like it really hurt. Come here and I'll kiss the boo-boo~
Owch! Look, I'm fine, I just need a minute to... Wait, I just remembered something!
I've heard of an experimental technology that puts you in an ultra-vivid dream state! It even lets multiple people experience the dream concurrently!
Though, this can't be it. If we moved our dream bodies, we'd bump into things in real life! That leaves but one option - this IS a dream after all...
Stop it, Kiev. Pulling her cheeks again won't do anything but hurt her.
One thing's for sure - when we manage to escape, I'll have to write a report on that machine.
Good idea. Omit the fact I asked for vodka and write about that instead.
As for the more immediate present... Does anyone know where we are?
The temperature's cozy, but the humidity will make my machines rust up. I'm guessing this is some island in the southern hemisphere?
Most likely. With the sea stretching on forever... Volga, can you launch some recon planes?
Sure can! I'll have a look-see at our surroundings~
Hmm... There's another island in the distance.
Don't see anything resembling a military installation... But there are lots of people in tourist clothes. Safe to say, this is a vacation destination!
You're kidding! So I was right with my first guess!
That puts us no closer to figuring out how we got here when we were in a submarine at the bottom of the sea just minutes ago, though.
It's a pleasant change of scenery, don't get me wrong, but a lot of things don't feel right here.
Well, there are no enemies or danger in sight, so I guess this is fine...?
...Kronshtadt, what do we do?
The briefing said to "gather data" and didn't elaborate beyond that.
Likely in no small part because Soyuz couldn't predict what would happen after we synchronized...
But don't worry, I'll get us out of this. Wherever we are, nobody alive expects us to be here. A spy like me couldn't ask for better circumstances than that.
Very true! You are a master at gathering your bearings in unfamiliar situations, after all!
Wait! You just admitted to being a spy! Not going to lie, you had this genius mechanic fooled for the longest time into thinking you weren't!
...Perhaps the machine malfunctioned. If it did, I'm glad it at least sent us to a vacation destination. Could've been much worse.
At any rate, we won't learn anything by standing around. Let's start by heading to the island Volga saw.
(This whole place feels fishy. Rather than put my suspicions to rest, this tropical setting only raises them...)

Richard, the One and Only.say: Hey, you there! That area's off-limits!
The Northern Parliament girls were exploring the foreign island when suddenly they were stopped by a stranger.
Back off!
Don't shoot, Kiev! She's a civilian!
...Sorry. Instinct took over.
Gosh, I'm terribly sorry! I hope we didn't scare you.
Nah, it's all good. My bad for calling out to you out of nowhere.
The stranger emerged from a roadside thicket, revealing herself. She was dressed much differently to them.
Plus, I'm not a civilian. I don't scare that easily
Interesting uniform... Do you mind telling us your name and where we are?
My name's Bon Homme Richard, and this place is Samos Island.
Curious pronunciation. Are you from Iris Libre or the Vichya Dominion?
I get that a lot, but nope, I was born in the Eagle Union and work for the Federation.
Oh, I see. And you said Samos just now? So we're in the Aegean Sea?
Not exactly. We're in the seas of Bermuda.
Sounds to me like you've lost your bearings. Did you sneak past the filter to play in the sun like me?
That's one way of putting it.
Yeah, I getcha. Sneaking around's pretty fun.
You're... not going to ask us to introduce ourselves?
I mean, your uniforms make it pretty obvious you're from the Northern Parliament.
Based on how disoriented you sound, I'll assume you suddenly found yourselves here with no clue how it happened.
In other words, same as what happened to me!
And I've got no reason to report you unless you do something totally unjustified. So just find a beach to lie down on and take it easy!
"No clue how it happened?" Excuse me?
(Psst. Let me take it from here.)
Let's rewind a bit. You said, "That area's off-limits" earlier. Why?
Is there more to this place than a tropical resort?
THIS place is a tropical resort, yeah, but keep going that-a-way and you'll arrive at the Samos Marine Research Institute.
You don't wanna go there. They had an accident a while ago which released ungodly beasties into the water. So I've heard, anyway.
I don't know what really went down, but my advice is to steer clear of that place.
Why hasn't anyone put up warning signs? One oblivious tourist is all it takes to cause a PR nightmare.
That's... a good question! Paradoxically though, tourism to this place has skyrocketed 'cause of that accident.
All people hoping to snap a picture of an urban legend, I presume?
Not all of them, but a large majority, yeah.
Anyway, there's no issue as long as you don't go there. Stick to this area and you'll be fine.
Thanks for the warning. One more question...
You also said we "snuck past the filter," just like you, yes?
Yup, it's just that I got here earlier than you. Speaking of which, I've actually gotta go soon.
Professor's got me on a strict schedule, giving me the ol' "it's only temporary" consolation, but it's hard getting used to.
Eh, forget it. Don't wanna ruin the wonderful weather with my grumbling!
Before I go though, wanna take a group picture with me?
Only natural to want to capture the moment in time you went to a beautiful place and made new friends, right?
(A group picture...? What's the deal with this girl? How do we proceed?)
(She'll distrust us if we refuse. Just go with it.)
(Marvelous! I've been waiting for a chance to show off my genius mechanic look! ...Come on, smile for the camera, Kiev!)
(Like this? Hope this is fine...)
Don't blink! 3, 2, 1!
Say "cheese!"
Cheese!
Niiice! And by the way, I won't be sharing this pic with anyone. It's just a little keepsake.
Man, I'm running short on time... What are you girls gonna do? Stay and have another look around?
Most likely. There's still more to see.
All right, have a good one~
Just stay away from the exercise area, or you're going to have a bad time.
If anyone asks, just say you got lost or suffered rigging malfunctions.
Huh? What did you just say?
Nothing! Just talking to myself! You guys would be nervous too if someone started asking you personal questions.
Now it's time for me to go. Peace out~
The girls bade farewell to Bon Homme Richard, who went back the way she came and disappeared into the wilderness.
I clearly heard that girl say something about a rigging... Kronshtadt, do you think she's...?
A shipgirl, yes. From the Eagle Union, at that.
...I shouldn't have let her take that picture. It could be used against us somehow...
How sloppy of me, damnit...
W-well... Hindsight is 20/20, so don't be too hard on yourself. All we can do is hope she really won't report us.
We haven't done anything totally unjustified in this place, so we'll have to take her word for it now that she's left!
Wait... She said she's FROM the Eagle Union but works for the Federation... What was that about?
Who knows. More importantly, what should we investigate next? The marine institute, or that exercise area she mentioned?
Good question. Both are potential leads, and we'll likely want to visit each anyway.
What do you think, Soobrazitelny?
My vote goes to the exercise area! That whole thing about monsters in the water sounds like a fabricated story to bring in tourists!
If it wasn't made up, they would at the very least put fences to keep people out! There's not even a warning sign to be seen!
So, I say we go to the exercise area where we can fully leverage our abilities!
You have a point... Riggings were built for use on the open sea, not inside a building on land. Exercise area it is.

Washed Away.say: BEEP BEEP––!! BEEP BEEP––!!
What the–? My communicator's going off!
Who could be... This is odd. I've never seen this frequency or comms channel used before.
Mine's quiet though. Let me guess, you jailbroke your communicator?
Isn't it obvious? What is a genius mechanic without a custom device? Ahh-ha-ha-haah!
Answer it. Whoever's calling, they might give us some valuable intel.
As you say!
You are entering a Federation exercise area. State your name and affiliation at once!
"Federation?" I think that's the organization Richard said she belongs to.
I've got a hunch they have ties to the Eagle Union. They might even be the Eagle Union under a different name.
That'll make this easier. Tell them the truth. If we're in luck, they might patch you through to the Commander and we can bypass Naval HQ.
Right, I'll do just that!
This is Northern Parliament destroyer Soobrazitelny and company! We hear you loud and clear!
We weren't aware this was an exercise area – apologies! If you'll tell us the boundary of it, we'll change course right away!
You're from the Northern Parliament?
Huh. They hung up.
As soon as I said we're from the Northern Parliament, too. Talk about rude.
Or maybe they dropped their phone in awe when they realized it was me, the genius mechanic, they were talking to!
I don't think they've ever heard of you.
Heads up! Several aircraft, 1 o'clock! They're headed straight for us!
Shit! Volga, scramble your planes! Everyone, spread apart and enter diamond configuration!
Why are they attacking us? Do they have us confused for their exercise opponent?
After I literally just told them we're with the Northern Parliament? Highly unlikely!
It has to be a misunderstanding. Otherwise why would they point their guns at people allied with the Azur Lane?
I'll try to contact them again... Gah, I can't! The channel's been deactivated!
Then we're in for a rough reception! Volga, give us aerial support!
Their planes are moving unbelievably fast, but I'll do my best!
The sky and the sea are... changing...!
Look! What is that?!
Kiev pointed up at the sky, obscured entirely by dark clouds when the weather had been clear just minutes ago.
The distant air churned and formed a black tornado, kicking up raging streams as it plowed towards the girls at staggering speeds.
A tornado too?! And those black waves...!
The tornado's rapid formation sent a ripple across the sea, growing into a towering tsunami. Only moments later, they came crashing down on the girls.
Everyone! Get behind me at–
Urgh... Aghh...! AGH! So this is it...
The crushing impact of the water sent Kronshtadt reeling. Not long after, she blacked out.

Into the Abyss II.say: FATAL ERROR.
██████ PROGRESS: 22.8%
Kronshtadt! KRONSHTADT!
Urgh...
Phew... Thank god I was able to pull you out in time.
*cough* *cough*... Ughh...
What's wrong? You look like you just went through hell.
Where... am I...?
Inside the Suliko's synchronization chamber. Do you need medical attention?
Now I remember... I was... synchronizing...
Goodness, the machine really did a number on you, both physically and mentally.
I know we joked about vodka a second ago, but I think you've earned a shot.
Like I said, it really was just a joke. Save the drinks for later.
Anyway... What the hell was that?
Hm? What the hell was what?
The black tornado. Don't you remember?
Black tornado? I really have no idea what you're talking about.
Forget it then. Let me ask you a different question: have you heard of a technology that puts you in a lifelike dream with other people?
Look, now's not the time for riddles. We're in the middle of a mission still.
Yeah, I suppose... The synchronization process never even started, did it?
No. It was about to, but I interrupted it immediately when you started tossing and turning in the chair.
Are you sure you're all right? We can stop altogether if you feel sick.
I'm fine, honestly. I just freaked out a bit. I'm ready to try again.
Everything's good on our end, Kronshtadt! You can start the synchronization process now!
Roger. Arkhangelsk, you ready to start again?
Yup. Let me do one final check. Artifact is in place... Computer systems online... Data transfer port set to open...
Kronshtadt put the headset on and waited in total darkness as Arkhangelsk worked in the background.
Did I hallucinate all of that? It felt so real...
All right. Initiating in 3... 2... 1... Now!
Don't blink! 3, 2, 1!
Was that–?!

Reboot.say: Several mass-produced icebreakers worked tirelessly to push through the thick ice, a small search fleet behind them.
The fleet was lead by Sovetskaya Belorussiya, sister ship to Sovetsky Soyuz.
We're approaching the coordinates of the distress signal sent from the research base.
Had it not been for the heroism of Rossiya and the Commander, we wouldn't even have access to these waters.
But, where could a submarine of that size be hiding...?
I've been looking around as well, but this is in fact where the signal came from.
I really hope it's not coming from the bottom of the sea...
Ice choked the horizon, spanning as far as the eye could see. As time passed, the participants in the search operation found it harder and harder even to make small talk.
I've found the source of the signal!
The signal led to the research base, an imposing structure rebuilt from a Siren facility. It was here that the "rescue signal" – and the location of the meteorite, would be traced once more.
So this is the research base that Soyuz had been telling me about. It looks even more impressive than I had imagined.
I was convinced that she was exaggerating when describing it. Turns out, she had not even done it justice.
There's nothing left in the research base.
This place has been completely covered in snow and ice through the passage of time...
I'm sorry... Kronshtadt, Arkhangelsk, Soobrazitelny, Kiev, and Volga...
I couldn't do anything for you back then...
But please hang in there... We're coming to bring you home...!

Holiday Getaway II.say: Kronshtadt's mind floated through a pure white void.
Moments ago she lay in an uncomfortable chair inside a dreary room. Now it was as though she'd left her own body and soared through the sky.
The experience felt a lot like downing an entire barrel of vodka then getting carted through a desert of snow and ice.
All of a sudden, she was blasted with countless packets of data. Images coalesced into a pixelated mess and overlapping voices became indiscernible from static noise.
How long was she exposed to that storm of information? Seconds? Hours? There's no telling.
But throughout it all, she clung on to her consciousness, lest the storm engulf her. Soon she could feel her own two feet touch the ground.
Ugghh... Why do I have the mother of all hangovers when I've not had a drop to drink...
Getting drunk is the fun part, and I've skipped straight to the horrible day after part...
(Wait a second... I've felt this exact thing before!)
(This is no straightforward case of deja vu... I can still remember it all so vividly.)
Kronshtadt?! H-how did this happen?!
(There's Arkhangelsk, saying the exact same thing as before...)
(Either she's just as surprised as last time, or I'm reliving the same dream only I can see...)
(Let's play along for now and see what happens.)
That's my line! I'm the one who was in the synchronization chair!
Yeah, it makes no sense... The machine started whirring, and the next thing I know, I'm here.
And we're not the only ones. Look! Soobrazitelny, Kiev, and Volga all wound up here too!
Mrmhh... Whuh– Where are we....?
Based on the climate and ocean currents, it seems we've wound up in the SA Ocean – somewhere near Bermuda.
What? Did you just figure that out in less than a minute? Because wow.
Please. Being able to make accurate deductions based on your surroundings is a required skill in my line of work.
(If Soobrazitelny was right, that means I'm the only dreamer here. That makes my objective finding out why I'm having this particular dream.)
(I need more info. Who is Richard really? What is that exercise area? How did that black tornado form?)
I've figured it out! This is all a dream! A dream where we're in a holiday resort somewhere!
Kiev, pinch my cheek as hard as you can!
No, don't. Let her do that and it'll be sore for days.
Days?! Never mind then, and thank you Kronshtadt! ...Also, aren't you a little too calm, given the nonsensical circumstances?
I'm fine with calm. I feel safe with a calm leader.
But how?! Even I lost my cool a minute ago, and I'm the most composed genius mechanic on the planet!
(It's not so hard when you've been through it once. Let's skip ahead a bit...)
Seems we were teleported here somehow. Volga, can you launch some recon planes?
There should be an island off in that direction. See if you can find it.
Okey-dokey. Give me just one minute...
...Wow, you're right! There IS an island there!
(Same place as last time. Safe to say the environment is fixed, then.)
Well, since the briefing simply said to gather data, that island is a good place to start.
My gut tells me it's connected to the meteorite somehow. Let's see if I'm right.

Reunion with Richard.say: Over on the island, the girls followed the tourist road until they came across a place with a thicket.
If memory serves me right, it's just up ahead...
Now she should pop out after we turn this right corner...
Who? How do you know someone else is here?
(Crap! Spoke my thoughts out loud!)
You're scaring Volga – she says something's felt wrong about you since shortly after we got here.
Really now? What makes her think that?
I dunno. Kiev and Soobrazitelny haven't said anything, and I didn't notice it either until Volga pointed it out.
But, Volga's intuition tends to be really good when it comes to people's emotions. That's why I wanted to check on you.
The fact that you're so calm and decisive in this strange situation... I'm not sure whether it's totally in character or totally out of character for you.
...I'm not sure what to make of that.
See, our intelligence personnel are trained to handle unknown situations, but that's not you, right?
That doesn't sound like a compliment to me.
In other words, I'm the one who should be saying, I'm not sure what to make of your behavior.
You've been acting weird from the moment you sat down in that chair.
Don't you think you can open up to us? Your friends are getting worried.
I... I'm sorry, but I still haven't sorted things through in my own head yet.
Just give me a bit more time...
All right. I'm not going to push you for answers, but we're in this together.
Hey, you there! That area's off-limits!
The conversation between Kronshtadt and Arkhangelsk was suddenly interrupted by a young girl's voice.
(Right on schedule.)
Back off!
Stand down, Kiev. I'll handle this.
...You sure that's a good idea?
Just do as I say. Now... Bon Homme Richard, I presume? I hope we didn't frighten you.
Nah, it's all good. My bad for calling out to you out of nowhere.
You know my name, huh? This is the last place I expected to run into one of my fans!
The stranger emerged from a roadside thicket, revealing herself. She was dressed much differently to them.
What are you be doing all the way out here? Is there something going on in that exercise area up ahead? Oh, and do you mind if I just call you Richard?
Woah, that's a lot of questions out of the blue... Say, are you girls spies from the Northern Parliament?
No, we're not! At least I'm no spy - there's no room for other stuff in the brain of a genius mechanic!
Haha, I was just cracking a joke. Nobody would hire a bunch of oddballs like you to be spies!
Sorry about that. It's just, those questions you asked me earlier came off a bit suspicious, you know?
Normally when you meet someone, you don't just go right into "what are you doing here," right?
But since you're interested, why don't we play a little game and have you guess what I'm doing here~♪
Hmm... How about, you snuck past the filter to have some fun in the sun? How does that sound?
Woow, you really got it right on the first try!
Bingo, bingo! I snuck away and came to visit this island!
But, I can't stay out too long or people will get antsy.
As for this exercise area... It's funny to me that you even know what it is.
You know what's really odd? That the Federation and the Revolutionary Front – both at odds with each other – are conducting huge exercises here. Same place, same time.
Oh, but I'm sure there are a bunch of other strong ships here! Are you going to participate as well?
I'm afraid not. We're just here as tourists.
(This is new... She mentioned the Federation before, but what is this "Revolutionary Front" organization?)
Before I go though, wanna take a group picture with me?
Only natural to want to capture the moment in time you went to a beautiful place and made new friends, right?
(A group picture...? What's the deal with this girl? How do we proceed?)
I don't mind. Sounds like a good idea to me.
(Marvelous! I've been waiting for a chance to show off my genius mechanic look! ...Come on, smile for the camera, Kiev!)
(Like this? Hope this is fine...)
Don't blink! 3, 2, 1!
Say "cheese"!
Cheese!
Man, I'm running short on time... What are you girls gonna do? Stay and have another look around?
Most likely. There's still more to see.
Fair enough. Don't you think it's boring just chilling on this island though?
You're still curious about that exercise area, aren't you? Ehehe. If you want to see for yourself what true "power" looks like––
Why don't you head down there yourselves? Hehe~♪
(What the hell is going on?! She's acting like a completely different person than before...)
Are you sure you're fine with that? Your "Federation" isn't going to find people like us suspicious?
Why? There's nothing wrong with girls who want "power."
Now it's time for me to go. Peace out~
The girls bade farewell to Bon Homme Richard, who went back the way she came and disappeared into the wilderness.
What a weird girl. Someone you know, Kronshtadt?
There are too many things to keep track of that I've never heard of before. What is this "Federation," and what's the deal with this exercise area?
(But what I'm more curious about... is why none of you seem to know anything.)
Sorry, but I've been totally lost since the moment I got here.
Sigh... You really are a true professional, Kronshtadt.
So, what do we check out next? Want to head down to the exercise area like that girl said?
The way she was talking about power in quotes was giving me the chills.
But did you see the smartphone she had on her? It looked insanely advanced!
Mechanical marvels are the only power that this genius mechanic needs! Let's stay away from that exercise area.
Or so I'd like to say, but if they really do have some ridiculous power, we should at least find out what it is.
I also want to see it for myself...
What are those two "at odds with each other" organizations she mentioned? I'm not sure they'd appreciate outsiders intruding into their exercise...
We'll have to move forward really carefully and avoid stepping on any toes then. Arkhangelsk, Volga, can we count on you two for the reconnaissance again?
Sure thing. Ready whenever you are.
Let's make good use of our time. Get going, everyone!

No Response.say: Tracing a course plotted by Kronshtadt, the girls advanced towards the exercise area located in the seas of Bermuda.
Soobrazitelny, has your communicator gone off in the past few minutes?
Nope! Not a single peep across any channels!
Good. That means we're making progress.
(Just going around the Federation's exercise area seems to be working. If all goes well, we'll be safe once we contact an Eagle Union fleet in the region.)
(I believe their closest base is situated on the east coast...)
After letting Soobrazitelny fine-tune her communicator, Kronshtadt contacted the Eagle Union base through a private channel.
(No response?! I'll try their SA base instead...)
She tried again and again with many different channels, but not a single one answered her calls.
(How is this possible? Is this not Eagle Union territory? Richard did say she's from the Eagle Union though, and all these tourists look to be as well...)
(I suppose this dream might be an alternate reality where the Eagle Union controls much less territory than it does in the real world.)
(That leaves us with little choice but to try with that "Revolutionary Front" faction... Hopefully they'll receive us more kindly than Federation did.)

Washed Away II.say: BEEP BEEP––!! BEEP BEEP––!!
What the–? My communicator's going off!
Who could be... This is odd. I've never seen this frequency or comms channel used before.
Mine's quiet though. Let me guess, you jailbroke your communicator?
Isn't it obvious? What is a genius mechanic without a custom device? Ahh-ha-ha-haah!
(What?! But we're well beyond the Federation's exercise area boundary! Who's calling us?)
(They'll light us up if we identify as belonging to the Northern Parliament... The best move here is to just ignore the call.)
(Ugh. Working covertly is so not my style, despite me being a spy...)
Ignore it. Close the comms channel and circle around to the other side of the sector.
Roger that!
Heads up! Mass-produced ships incoming, straight ahead!
Are they Sirens?
No. They're most similar to Northern Parliament ships, but they don't look right...
They've got camouflage and profiles unlike any I've seen before. I'm not sure they're on our side!
Then they probably aren't! They must belong to either the Federation or the Revolutionary Front!
I want to get a closer look at them! Kronshtadt, permission to advance!
Bad idea. What would you do if a potentially dangerous shipgirl approached you without warning?
Good point! I'll give them a heads-up through my communicator then!
That said, it does feel pretty strange to contact them now after we ghosted them just earlier!
(Dammit, so we have to answer someone's call in the end... Is there no way to travel through here without getting into trouble?)
While the girls debated how to contact the Revolutionary Front, the sector the mass-produced ships came from underwent a drastic transformation.
The sky and the sea are... changing...!
Look! What is that?!
Kiev pointed up at the sky, obscured entirely by dark clouds when the weather had been clear just minutes ago.
As though the mass-produced ships too were bewildered by the unexpected abnormal weather, they abruptly broke formation and changed targets.
They're firing their cannons – but not at us! They're shooting into the clouds!
They cycled through their volleys, launching barrage after barrage into something concealed within a black tsunami, growing in size over the horizon.
Explosions boomed, gunfire flared, and smoke rose – after which a storm began taking shape from the black seas, a chaotic mass clawing at the sky.
(It's happening all over again... There's the black tornado!)
The distant air churned and formed a black tornado, kicking up raging streams as it plowed towards the girls at staggering speeds.
Far faster than the streams were the waves generated by the tornado's formation. They would come down on the girls in just moments from now.
Brace for impact! Destroyers, get behind us bigger ships! Volga, Arkhangelsk, hurry!
(I'm not letting them wipe us out a second time!)
(I WILL persist, find out who's creating this black tornado, burn their identity into my memory, and learn the truth behind this dream if it's the last thing I do!)
The relentless black waves whipped Kronshtadt's entire body, each impact stronger than the last, pushing her stamina to its limits.
Each hit bore down on her like a sub-zero slash and burned like the flame of a blowtorch, wearing away at her rigging, her body, and her spirit bit by bit.
Her consciousness began to slip. The hard truth dawned on her – there was no overcoming the black tornado and the destructive waves it sent forth.
Argh! The pain is unbearable... Even worse than the last time...!
But... my persistence... paid off! I now know... who's creating the tornado...!
The mass-produced ships that dotted the horizon had been completely obliterated, leaving only a roaring maelstrom sitting atop a throne of shattered hulls.
Water, flames, hulls, and smoke – nothing was spared from its ravenous maw. Its destructive energy poured out endlessly in all directions.
When Kronshtadt turned her eyes to the center of the maelstrom, it was then that she came face-to-face with "her."
Though the figure seemed so small in comparison to the swirling chaos, there was nothing weak about her appearance. From the storm seemed to echo familiar laughter.
You... You're the one responsible for this...!
Kronshtadt etched that thought into her mind just before her consciousness was once again wrenched away from her.

Into the Abyss III.say: FATAL ERROR.
██████ PROGRESS: 37.1%
Kronshtadt? Kronshtadt! Are you all right?
Thank god... You're finally awake.
Urgh......
How are you feeling? Any pains, aches, or nausea?
(I'm back in the synchronization chamber... Must mean the black tornado got me again.)
Everything from the dream was gone. The mass-produced ships, the tornado, and the being who created the storm.
Gone, like they were never real to begin with.
Arkhangelsk, let's put the synchronization on hold for a bit.
Look how pale you are! I knew this was a bad idea. One moment you're fine, the next you're squirming like you're having a waking nightmare.
Sigh... Sorry. I should've given you vodka when you first requested it.
I'll get you a glass. Wait right here.
Would you please forget about the goddamn vodka?
Shh, don't talk, just get some rest. I'll tell Soobrazitelny we're taking a break.
You might FEEL fine, but you're not fit for this mission without a sound mind. And you, my friend, are not sound of mind right now.
(This confirms it – I'm the only dreamer between us five. No wonder Arkhangelsk has no idea what's really going on here.)
(Huh...? Her hands are shaking...)
Why are you staring a hole through me? Is something wrong?
(No, I was too quick to judge. She also experienced what happened in the dream too, she simply doesn't remember it.)
(I'm getting weaker with each time the tornado consumes me. The other four must be feeling it too...)
(This machine is extremely dangerous. We'll all die if I keep using it. No, what we need to do is return to the base immediately.)
(But... how? Without weapons, we're just as dead if we encounter any Sirens on the way. Plus, the meteorite has to stay here...)
Kronshtadt? Kronshtadt, listen to me. It's important.
This isn't your fight alone. Your friends are right here with you, and we'll always listen when you have something on your mind.
(She's right. Try as I might, I can't do this on my own. I need some time away from this claustrophobia-inducing room.)
Sorry, I need to get some fresh air. Fresher than what's in this chamber, at least.
Suliko Interior - Bridge
(We are in some deep shit here. Things aren't looking good.)
(Analyzing the meteorite is sapping our strength. Running away runs the risk of encountering Sirens. Even if we get away, we'll never get this golden opportunity again.)
(If this is a side-effect of the connection, then there's no way Soyuz could've foreseen its effects...)
(Heh... Looks like I really jinxed us trying to be a blowhard back then...)
Now, I'd like to reiterate – we likely won't get another opportunity like this.
The Northern Parliament's future rests on your shoulders, everyone. Bear that in mind all throughout this mission.
Good thing this is exactly the kind of mission I've been looking forward to.
(Cursing myself won't do us any good. I need to come up with a plan. Think, Kronshtadt, think...)
There you are, Kronshtadt! I took the liberty of locking the grappling arms in position so I don't need to babysit the controls.
If it isn't our genius captain. How are things?
Pretty good! Working a submarine is a walk in the park when you're a genius mechanic!
I'll bet. Where are Kiev and Volga?
You're sitting right next to them. Don't tell me the machine messed with your head?
Kronshtadt...?
...Oops, sorry. I guess I'm a little out of it right now.
A little? With how ill you look, it's a miracle you can still walk.
If you need someone to synchronize in your place, I'm willing to do it.
But if you insist on doing it yourself, please pace yourself. There's no need to rush when the Sirens are still unaware of us.
You know what... I'll take your advice and take a little break.
But nobody besides me will synchronize. Soyuz entrusted this mission to me, and I won't let her down.
(Besides, poor Volga would be mentally scarred for life if she felt what it's like to be swallowed by that black tsunami.)
I do appreciate the concern, but I can handle myself. You worry too much, Volga.
Do I? Maybe you're right. I've wondered a few times if I'm making myself useful enough, and feared the mission might not be going as planned...
That's just me being me though. With the meteorite in our possession, we only need to extract all the data we can, then we'll be home free.
We're not home free until we're home, period! Making it back safely is part of the mission!
Personally, I've been enjoying every minute on this submarine! I'm having too much fun to let doubts cloud my mind!
Me, I wish I was fighting Sirens right now. Studying rocks is boring.
Having said that... I'd rather be here with my friends than out fighting alone.
Incidentally, Kronshtadt, I wanted to ask this earlier but you were busy: could we stop by the research base for a while longer when we go back?
I know it has no tech left to salvage, but there's no shortage of machines I'd love to tinker with!
Urgh......
Sure, sure. I'll mention your request to Soyuz later.
Keep in mind though that the biggest toll this mission takes is on our minds. If you don't want to be left alone without proper utilities, you might want to reconsider.
I wouldn't want to be all alone! Let me bring Kiev with me!
...No way, no how. I've got combat training to do, and I'd hate to neglect it.
You can still do that! You said a minute ago you'd rather fight with friends on your side, remember?
Sirens already appeared on the way here, and I'll bet more will show up at the base eventually!
It's the best of both worlds! You and I can hang out and fight together!
That's... actually a good point.
On second thought, I'm fine with that. Kronshtadt, you can put me on base guard duty after the mission.
No promises, but I'll see what I can do.
All right, I think I've rested for long enough. Wouldn't want to keep Arkhangelsk waiting all day.
(Thank you all – for helping me regain my sanity.)
Arkhangelsk! Are you good to go?
Yes, and you don't need to shout. Any louder and the Sirens would've probably heard it.
Jokes aside, hearing you shout is a good sign – means you've regained your strength. Ready to give synchronization another shot?
Of course. I can't lounge around too long or I get restless.
Let's do this. Take me to dreamland once more.

Holiday Getaway III.say: After being blinded by a bright white light, the girls awaken once again, having been transported to the seas of Bermuda.
(Starting point is the same as last time. Good.)
Kronshtadt?! H-how did this happen?!
Huh...? How'd I get here? I was on the bridge just a moment ago...
What is happening here?! Did I doze off and start dreaming?! Kiev, come here and pinch my cheek!
Oh no... I don't like this one bit...
Those same clear blue skies. The warm sunshine. The tranquil sound of the sea. This place was seemingly the idyllic holiday resort.
And in less than a few hours, all of it would be engulfed by that horrific black tsunami.
That is, unless someone put a stop to it first.
(After all this, it's time to let them in on the truth.)
Everyone calm down and listen. This is now our third time in this place. Let me explain...
You have got to be kidding me! Your story has too many holes in it! It's simply not possible!
That's a bit too farfetched even for me to believe, I'm afraid...
It's impressively elaborate for a story you made up to ease our minds, I'll give you that much.
It's not, Arkhangelsk. I swear on my honor and reputation that it's the truth. You have to believe me.
Well... You are the leader, and I don't think you'd lie to us.
Assuming you're telling us the truth, that still leaves some unanswered questions.
You said on the first visit we were engulfed by a black tsunami, interrupting the synchronization process and sending us back to the Suliko.
Then the second visit, the tsunami got us again and the synchronization process was interrupted.
And now it's our third visit, and yet you're the only one with any recollection of what happened. Why?
Hmm... Wait, I know! The answer has been right under our noses!
It's the headset! Only Kronshtadt has worn it, right?
It must somehow let her retain her memories of what happened in this place! Without it, you forget it all!
Now, that doesn't explain why the rest of us get dragged into the dream as well, but my hypothesis is that the machine "hooks on" to the mind of anyone within range and drags them in!
Then when it disconnects, it takes your memory of the dream with it, but the headset, dare I say, synchronizes your real and dream memories!
It's a stretch, but it does connect the dots.
Even if we forget everything that happens in here, we'll always trust you.
Just explain it like you did just now, and we'll believe you no matter how many loops we forget!
Yeah... It'll be fine.
Thank you all for believing me. I'll explain it all as many times as needed, but let's try to finish the mission on this loop.
You know, now that I'm aware this is all a dream, suddenly I can't help but feel creeped out by this place.
After processing your explanation a little, some things you mentioned stood out to me.
You said during the first and second visits, that Richard girl looked different, but everything else was the same.
That, to me, suggests she plays a pivotal role in this dream. Hell, her appearance might continue changing.
Then there's that black tornado – and tsunami – that sends us back to the Suliko when it gets us. That's the failure condition, right?
What we should do then is pursue options that extend the dream for as long as possible while we gather new information.
Easy. We travel away from the exercise area and explore some other place.
Good idea! And if that Richard girl appears, we should just avoid her altogether. I don't trust her.
I'm in favor of a change of pace from the last loops and traveling the opposite direction! We aren't far from the Eagle Union's east coast!
If we get far enough away from the exercise area, the black tsunami won't reach us even if it does manifest!
Plus, we can observe it from a safe distance and gather data! What do you say?
You make a good case... Let's do it. Third time's the charm.
Once the waves have subsided, we can take our sweet time exploring this place.
Ahh-ha-ha-haah! A genius always makes genius suggestions! Now, let us set off!

Off Script.say: Taking a different approach to the last two loops, this time the girls sailed away from the exercise area and towards the Eagle Union's east coast.
...Should happen any minute now.
What should?
I kept an eye on the clock since we arrived, and it's around this time the black tsunami appears.
Had we done the same as before, it'd be rushing towards us right now.
But we're not seeing one, soooo... does that mean we did it? Did we get around the tsunami?
Hell yes! My plan worked!
Don't pop the champagne just yet. That tornado and subsequent tsunami are so massive they can be seen for miles and miles.
So if they do form despite us being nowhere near, we'll see it.
Uhh, yeah, about that... It's getting pretty foggy pretty fast out here, isn't it?
So foggy... it's hard to see anything.
*inhale*... *exhale*... *inhale*...
What are you doing?
Practicing my breaststroke, just in case the tsunami reaches us!
...You won't swim your way through a tsunami, you know.
It's a joke, you're supposed to laugh! If Kronshtadt couldn't withstand that monster of a wave, I'm as screwed as a nut on a rocket hull!
On a serious note, this fog is getting thicker at an alarming rate. The horizon was clearly visible just a minute ago...
This is abnormal even for the Bermuda Sea. Someone has to be manipulating the weather.
For all we know, there could be Mirror Seas even in this dream world. Be on your guard, everyone.
Volga, can you see anything from above?
Sadly not... It's like the whole sea became covered in a layer of fog.
It'll be impossible to stay oriented like this...
Hey, you there! This area's off-limits!
Eek! Wh-wh-who the hell are you?!
Back off!
A girl outfitted with ship rigging suddenly emerged from the fog, right in front of the Northern Parliament girls.
Kiev responded immediately with a volley. The girl dodged and circled behind Soobrazitelny, who was still reeling from shock.
She melted into the fog again before making the same entrance, suddenly appearing right before Kronshtadt.
Richard...?
Yup, that's me!
Lower your weapons, everyone! Do not attack her!
Kronshtadt ordered her comrades to stand down before walking with a brave stride towards the girl only known as Bon Homme Richard.
Come on, lady. Is this any way to greet someone?
Why are you here?
Hey, that's what I'm wondering. Why are YOU people here?

Washed Away III.say: The outline of Bon Homme Richard was clearly visible even through the fog.
Though she was equipped with her rigging, she showed no signs of hostility, her demeanor disarming as always.
However, Kronshtadt viscerally felt something terrifying hidden beneath that smile.
The reason was that...
You're not the Richard I know. She didn't look like this.
Answer me: Who are you really?
You're a strange one, lady. You just called my name, didn't you?
Bon Homme Richard.
What...?! But how...
Hmm?
In Kronshtadt's head, the image of the girl standing before her shifted rapidly between the one she knew before, and the current one.
Arkhangelsk! Describe how this girl looks to you, right now!
Her hair color, eye color, and the length of her hair!
White, blue, and semi-long, respectively!
I'm seeing the same thing! Why, are you seeing something different?
What's going on? Why am I the only person seeing something different?!
But this world is my... My...
Answer me! Who the hell are you? And how are you messing with my mind?
You're looking at the real Bon Homme Richard, lady.
Actually... Heheheh, I'm more curious about how I appear to you through your eyes.
Richard continued to chuckle bemusedly, a sense of fear awakening within Kronshtadt.
She wracked her brain, sifting through her memories... Following the trails left behind through the dreams to anything pertaining to the person in front of her.
Right... That photo! We took a photo!
But... we didn't meet this time, so there wouldn't be a photo...
Oh, are you talking about that photo? I have it right here~
What...?
Kronshtadt looked at the photo Richard held in her hand.
Despite the familiar, friendly setting of the photo, the girl's inhuman red eyes gazed back at her with a gleeful emptiness.
This isn't what happened before... I'm sure of it...

She tried to stay calm, sorting through all the possibilities in her head. For someone so used to controlling all sorts of information, the inexplicable and illogical drove the seeds of fear deeper into her heart.
Are you okay, lady? You don't look so good~
(My comrades aren't noticing anything strange about Richard. I need to compose myself and get information out of her...)
I'm fine. Can you tell me about the restricted area up ahead?
Hm? Are you talking about this exercise area?
Exercise area? I thought we were heading in the opposite direction!
Ahaha, what are you talking about? This entire place is the exercise area! You wouldn't want to miss our exciting wargame, would you?
Well, not that you'd be able to anyway~
Look, it's about to start right now! Hehehe... Ahahahaha!
Suddenly, Richard's ordinarily amicable voice turned grating and maniacal.
I still don't know who you are or what you're after...
But if you intend to block our way, then we will show you no mercy!
Don't be so trigger-happy, lady. Hold it in a bit longer – there's plenty of fun to be had~♪
Though Kronshtadt already stood poised to start shooting, Richard continued to ramble mirthfully, as relaxed and carefree as ever.
(What's with this girl...? I feel as if I'm being watched... by thousands of people, not just this one girl...)
(Whatever she is, she's dangerous! And at this rate, we're going to become her prey!)
(We need to get out of here, fast... But how? Think, think!)
(That's it, that's the only answer. We have to defeat her. We have to defeat her and get out of here as quickly as possible!!)
Huh? Kronshtadt, what are you doing?
Everyone, fire! Unload everything you have into that girl!
KABOOOM!
Kronshtadt's main gun roared to life before she even finished her command.
Following her lead, the other members of her fleet started to unload into Richard as well.
...But, their attacks seemed to pass right through her body, incapable of leaving even a dent.
Time's up~♪
Heheh... Hehehe... Ahahaha! Ahhahahahahahahaha!
It's starting! Yes, yeeees! It's starting again! Ahahahahaha!
The girl's cacophonous laughter seemed to split through the fog covering the area in the blink of an eye.
Under a sky darkened by black clouds, countless mass-produced ships stood lined up, preparing to face each other.
What? Where'd all those ships come from?!
C'mon, lady, let's have lots and lots of fun!
The girl spread her arms wide, pitch-black water coalescing around her in a roaring vortex.
Peals of thunder and flashes of lightning greet her dark storm, the same black maelstrom re-forming around her.
Kronshtadt fought to suppress the fear tickling her throat back into her heart, clenching her fists tightly to muster what power she had.
The swirling mass in front of her could make the very ocean answer its sinister call. She steeled herself for the waves that would come to smash her consciousness to bits.
Everyone, get behind–
The force of the impact engulfed Kronshtadt entirely. But instead of being devoured by it...
"It... doesn't hurt...?"
"I see. This would be the third time... Maybe my body's gotten used to it..."
"Or does this mean... I've grown stronger?"
"This power flowing through me... It feels surprisingly... good."
"Ah... Imagine what I could do to my enemies with this power... How I'd love to sink them, annihilate them..."
"I am a weapon. I want to destroy everything... Missions, duties..."
"...My enemies... Where are they?"
*cough* *cough*... Everyone, are you all right?!
Urgh... Somehow, yeah... What in the world was that ridiculous tsunami? My body's hurting all over...
Wait, it's not over yet! We haven't been pulled back to the submarine yet!
I'm getting a bad feeling about this... We need to get out of here...
Seconded. My rigging's gonna come apart if I get by something like that again!
Kronshtadt, are you okay? Kronsh–
"Targets... sighted."
"...Yes, those are my enemies. Look, over there too."
Kronshtadt silently turned around and pointed the muzzle of her gun at her "enemies."
"Preparing... to engage the enemies..."
(No... What am I doing...?! Those are my friends!)
Her main gun discharged. Then her secondary guns. Every tool of violence on her rigging vomited fire and brimstone, an uncontrollable lust for destruction pouring out without end.
Something in front of her collapsed to the ground. That something desperately clutched at the hem of her clothes.
But she didn't stop shooting. She could not stop shooting. She could see nothing, hear nothing, feel nothing, think nothing.
Run... Run... away...!
Kronshtadt could only squeeze a few words out with the last bit of her strength before her consciousness was once again swallowed by darkness.

Into the Abyss IIII.say: FATAL ERROR.
██████ PROGRESS: 89.9%
Kronshtadt opened her eyes and scanned her surroundings. She was back in the Suliko's synchronization chamber.
We just... barely made it out...
She rose to a sitting position and combed her hair in order with her hand.
...Arkhangelsk? Where are you?
Volga? Hello?
Kiev? Kiiieeev?
Even you, Soobrazitelny...?
Her friends were nowhere to be seen.
Strings of letters passed by on the screen in front of her while the faint whirring of a computer fan continued in the background.
I'm alone? Where did everyone go?
Suliko Interior - Bridge
There's no one here either... And it looks like the sub is set to autopilot.
She doesn't seem to be malfunctioning, at least. But I can't turn the autopilot off...
I'll try my communicator... Sigh, no response.
After searching every nook and cranny of the Suliko, Kronshtadt dragged her weary body back to where she started.
Ugh... Headache. Plus now I'm getting dizzy.
I'm the only one left. But I can't possibly pilot and maintain a submarine all by myself...
We... I have failed. The mission is a failure.
Seems my ultimate fate is to be written off as missing in action, along with the Suliko.
...What a miserable climax to my life.
Stranded at the bottom of the ocean, soon to be forgotten by everyone... And I deserve it for betraying my comrades.
She turned her gaze to the data on the screen. Ordinarily she'd feel like trying to decode it, but now, that felt meaningless to her.
...No, it can't end like this...
She remembered the glimpses she caught of Arkhangelsk before being sent into the dream, and the final step she did to initiate the synchronization process.
It's not too late. I'll finish this mission even I have to do it by myself...
I refuse to be forgotten, to perish as a traitor...!
The only thing worse than dying in that dream...
Is to do at the bottom of this abyss all alone!

Rescue.say: Suliko Interior - Bridge
Ugh... My head hurts...
Is this... the submarine's bridge?
Anyone alive? Kiev? Volga? Arkhangelsk!
...Too noisy. Shush.
Nnngh... Did I fall asleep?
Mm... I had a really lifelike nightmare...
We were trapped in a dense fog... then a black tornado appeared... After that, we were all knocked out by a huge wave...
Yeah, I had the same dream...
Woah, what are the odds of that? Did we all have the same dream?
Oh right, Kronshtadt said the same thing, didn't she? She was talking about being in a dream, then getting jolted back to reality by that black wave...
Now that you mention it... Were we able to come back to reality because we were swallowed by that wave?
That incredible pain... Kronshtadt must have endured it those other times as well...
But, why do I remember now? Shouldn't we forget about the dream after we come back...?
I'm not sure, but maybe Kronshtadt has some answers for us!
Suliko Interior - Synchronization Chamber
Kronshtadt?
She... doesn't seem to be conscious yet.
Arkhangelsk pointed to Kronshtadt's immobile figure within the synchronization chair.
What's going on...?
I don't know. When I woke up, I saw her next to me in this state...
What a strange dream... Do you remember anything, Arkhangelsk? Kronshtadt mentioned something about a black tsunami.
Yes, including what happened during the first two loops.
And, during this last loop, she...
Pointed her guns at us, right?
Yes... I remember that still...
That couldn't have been the real Kronshtadt! She wasn't acting like herself!
But more importantly, why hasn't she woken up yet? Don't tell me she's trapped inside that dream still!
I'd hate to be stuck in that place...
Hmm... Do you think we could just wake her up?
Something's telling me that would be a terrible idea.
Huh? Why?

The device likely interfaces with her brain during the connection process, right? So if we were to sever the connection forcefully, it might cause her psychological damage! Maybe there's a way to stop the process...
I already thought of that... But I can't get it to stop...
Ugh... So that's not an option either, huh? But we can't just leave Kronshtadt like this!
Wait, that's it! If Kronshtadt is trapped inside the dream, why don't we go back in and "finish" it for her?
Uhh... I don't think we have the time for that...
The Sirens are starting to resume their patrols in this area.
You've got to be shitting me! Did the Sirens notice us already?
Maybe we should head back to the research base? It's a bit of a gamble, but we can't just leave our comrade behind.
It's not a bad idea on paper, but the synchronization is only made possible by connecting with the meteorite. I have no idea what'll happen if that connection is broken...
Oh, right...
And didn't Kronshtadt mention the other problem as well? If the Sirens find us while we're ascending, we're goners!
If the submarine goes down, Kronshtadt's gonna be the least of our worries!
What should we do then? We still have plenty of supplies, but... can we only wait for help?
C'mon, genius mechanic, think! How do we all get out of here alive?
And how do we rescue Kronshtadt from her dream? Graaaaaaah!
All right, we've got to go the distance here. If ascending is not an option, there's only one way to go – deeper!
I dunno how much pressure this submarine can handle, but at least the meteorite's gonna be fine!
Every second we keep diving is another second that we can keep the Sirens from noticing us!
Not like there's anything else we can do... But here's the problem – even if we do manage to bring Kronshtadt back, then what?
Wait... I'm picking up a signal! An... An SOS signal?!
Huh? That's impossible, there's no way our comms would work at this depth...
Wait, what? Didn't we originally locate the meteorite thanks to a mysterious signal?
Does that mean it's coming from the meteorite then?! If we can find some way of amplifying it, then it'll be picked up by the research base, and our comrades will know about it!
Next, if we can increase the strength of the connection, we might be able to find a way back into Kronshtadt's dream!
Wait. Who's going to pilot the submarine then? If you strengthen the connection, we're all going to get pulled inside the dream, right?
H-haha... Time to pray that the sub's autopilot function actually works?
Well, maybe I can throw together a quick routine to help it avoid Siren detection... But it won't be good for long.
What other choice do we have at this point? If we don't try something bold and daring, we'll just be waiting for our doom!
Understood. How long does it take to do all of those things?
Heheh! Faster than you can say "Soobrazitelny, the galaxy-brained mechanic!"
Surrounded by enemies, the Northern Parliament fleet rushed to finalize their desperate measures.
Inside the synchronization chamber, Volga monitored Kronshtadt's condition while keeping an eye on the operation of the connection device.
On the bridge, Kiev was fine-tuning the Siren avoidance routine while Arkhangelsk focused on amplifying the meteorite's signal from inside the communication room.
In a monumental feat of diligence and discipline, the Northern Parliament ships worked together seamlessly to finish their preparations in no time.
With the completion of Soobrazitelny's command, nothing would keep them from their crucial mission.
All righty! That's a real genius mechanic for ya! Did you see that?
How about the signal from the meteorite? We're casually doing all kinds of revolutionary things here, but I hope Soyuz at least manages to get the signal...
There's no guarantee. All we can do is send out the signal to draw in anyone who's paying attention. Also, you never know what might bite.
Hopefully we'll have Kronshtadt back by then too...
I don't want to think about losing another comrade ever again...
Anyway, are you sure everything's ready to go, Soobrazitelny? We can take some time to double-check things if needed.
Way ahead of you! When I'm surrounded by such beautiful machines, it'd be a shame to perform only a single check on them!
Heh. I guess that's true. You still want to head back to the research base and take another look around, right?
Kronshtadt's going to have to put in a good word with Soyuz for you, so you'd better make sure she comes back to us safe and sound!
All right... Let's go bring her back, together!
Are we ready to connect then? Let's make ourselves comfortable first~
After confirming that all preparations were complete, Volga resumed the boot sequence of the connection device.
See you on the other side, my friends!

Annihilation Program.say: An alien scenery spread out before Kronshtadt's eyes.
...So this is the climax of the show.
Flames danced atop water. Twisted metallic headstones adorned the surface. Unworldly weather raged above the exercise area from before.
An abandoned sector of the sea, ravaged by an unfathomable storm that consumed everything in its path.
The grand fleet that had once dotted the horizon was now nothing more than a graveyard, a solemn witness to the calamity that had passed over it.
Flames, smoke, and blackened waters. Even the very air choked the senses in this apocalyptic hellscape.
Only one thing continued to dance in the gala of death and destruction.
It seems... alive.
No, it always was...
Flames and storms raging all around her, Kronshtadt took aim with her main guns as she approached the black tornado.
Kronshtadt knew she had to confront it. No matter how many tries it took or how much time passed.
If I defeat you, then the operation... My mission... can finally end...
She turned her eyes towards the figure at the heart of the maelstrom – Bon Homme Richard.
Kronshtadt could not see the girl. But she could feel her presence.
As if answering her, a laughter began to ring out in her head, both innocent and maniacal.
Defeat me? Why would you want to do that?
I must complete my mission.
Why? Is that mission really that important to you?
I will defeat you, put an end to this dream, and return to the Northern Parliament with my comrades.
Comrades? No, no, Kronshtadt. Don't you remember how you hunted them down yourself?
There is no camaraderie in this world. Only temporary alliances out of convenience.
Your Northern Parliament is no different. They only support you when they need something from you.
Friendship. Factions. Loyalty. You don't need those things. You don't need any of those things.
Shut your crazed, lying mouth!
I will defeat you, solve the mystery of this meteorite, and bring my comrades back...!
Hahah! Is that so? I see, I see.
Even if you manage to defeat me, all that proves is that you have great power.
But power is fearsome. That applies to countries, to factions, to organizations, and to people.
People seek power for themselves, but when they get it, all that power does is breed fear.
Isn't that ironic? Isn't that hilarious?
Face it, Kronshtadt. Even if you defeat me, no glory awaits you. Your "comrades" won't ever come back.
You are all alone. Forlorn. Shunned. Denounced. Destroyed.
No, you're wrong! You know nothing about my comrades or the Northern Parliament!
You are the one who knows nothing.
Everything is a lie, a convenient excuse to use you... to use the KAN-SEN.
Yes... Kindness, support, respect. It's all a lie.
The glories afforded to you are cheap tokens to buy your loyalty, good for nothing but manipulating you.
The moment they realize they are unable to control you...
Is the moment they will come for you to destroy you. After all, what is an unusable weapon but a heap of scrap metal?
If you still won't admit it, then show me that power.
Defeat me! Destroy me! ERASE MY EXISTENCE!

Critical Mass.say: Kronshtadt pumped shell after shell into the black tornado, resuming the symphony of destruction with a new movement.
"Die! Die! DIE!"
She had lost all control of her emotions, becoming a puppet to her pure lust for destruction, her existence reduced to unload her entire arsenal at her target.
The tornado, meanwhile, simply took it all. It did not retaliate or even defend itself.
(How laughable... Am I really trying to kill a storm?)
(It's a force of nature, not a living being. I'm making a fool of myself...)
(But... Pointless as it may be...)
Her assault did not let up.
Is that the best you can do, lady? You've not left as much as a dent on me!
"Shut up! You've not seen the start of it!"
Her identity was being overwritten. Her consciousness began to wane.
Soon destruction would be the only thing she could think of. Soon she would be no more than a living weapon.
Her comrades, her allegiance, her duty, her mission – everything she held dear was being reduced to forgotten memories.
Never before had something felt so reprehensibly wrong. And yet she couldn't stop firing. Her power grew and grew without limit.
"AHAHAHAHAHAHAH!"
"Prepare to meet your end! Drop dead already! DIEEEEE!"
Richard was right there, standing right in front of her.
With a smile like a fallen angel, she pressed her little head against the barrel of Kronshtadt's cannon.
You've almost got me, lady.
A little more should do it~♪
No... No! This isn't like me! I would never...!
Despite her rejection, she could not stop firing. The world turned pitch black, and she realized then that she was on the brink of METAmorphosis.
But then... a ray of light shone through to Kronshtadt.

Abyssal Refrain.say: (What... happened to me...)
The black tornado was no longer there. Nor were Richard, the wrecks, or the exercise area.
Kronshtadt instead found herself in a strange, concrete hall.
Columns of lamps illuminated her surroundings with a faint light.
(Maybe I've finally arrived at the gates of heaven...)
Kronshtadt wandered out of the hall without any sense of purpose, trudging into a corridor. Suddenly, a plain wooden door caught her attention.
(Huh, I wonder what's in here...)
The moment her fingertips brushed against the doorknob, the white concrete hall transformed into a dimly-lit room.
Books and scraps of paper littered the floor, and opposite her in this horribly cluttered room sat a woman at her desk, writing something.
I don't understand... How did this happen?
Her Wisdom Cube... Why did that happen...?
I've never seen anything like it before...
I don't understand... I just don't understand...
BANG! She swung her fist without thinking, knocking over a stack of books.
(Watch out!)
Kronshtadt instinctively rushed over to shield the woman from the falling books, but they slipped through her body and crashed all over the desk and the floor.
Damnit... That hurt...
If only I could see the test data... But I don't have any clearance anymore...
I'll have to count on Dr. Aoste then... and focus on doing what I can do right here!
Don't give up! If anyone can crack this problem, it's you, Anzeel!
Kronshtadt tried to reach out to the woman, but the moment she did so, the scenery around her warped and whisked her back to the previous hallway.
(Was that a researcher? What a slovenly lifestyle... Not that I have any right to judge others though...)
(I don't know who she is or what she's doing, but I hope she succeeds.)
Continuing down the corridor, she came across another door.
(What's behind this one? Another vision?)
This room was lit even more dimly than the last. Kronshtadt could just make out the silhouettes of an adult man and a young girl.
Magister, your calculation has been completed.
It's finally done?! What are the results?
The results indicate that the proposed safety lock mechanism is able to satisfy project specifications.
I see... This is great news. We've finally done it.
I will consider the task complete, then. However, I could perform further calculations if you would like a more stable alternative.
No need. What we've got now strikes the perfect balance between stability and energy output ratio.
Stability comes at the cost of output – limiting it too much would be to put the cart before the horse.
Do you not have faith in Antiochus as a contingency plan?
That's not what I'm saying. I'm just biased towards those girls, perhaps because Anzeel sees so much in them.
Her incessant gushing has piqued my curiosity. Now I, too, want to observe this "Awakening" process that unlocks their latent potential.
What of the Bon Homme Richard incident? Do you believe it to be an example of an Awakening?
Don't be ridiculous. That was the opposite of an Awakening – it was more akin to the girls reverting to their original selves.
That is to say, their Wisdom Cubes underwent the process of degenerating into Origin Cubes. Knowing Anzeel, she's brainstorming a snappy term for it as we speak.
And if she is, she really should be working on something more important.
You know how it goes. When Anzeel slacks, I have to watch her back.
Oh, that reminds me. It's about time I had a talk with our assistant.
When the conversation in the dimly-lit room wrapped up, Kronshtadt found herself back in the white corridor once more.
(Another researcher? Huh... Who was that "assistant" he mentioned?)
(Surely not the little girl? She looked young enough to be his daughter. Far too young to be a lab assistant.)
(Beyond that, I didn't get a word of what they were talking about. Sigh... If only Soobrazitelny were here. She could make sense of it.)
(Wait... If only who were here? Who's Soobrazitelny?)
(I've never heard that name before, and yet... It feels familiar, for some reason.)
(Soobrazitelny... Soobra...zitelny... Oh!)
Repeating the name buried in the back of her mind caused an unexpected third door to appear right before her.
The vision on the other side seemed to be set in some sort of assembly hall, or a theater.
Should we leave some sort of message here?
This is the final stage of the safety lock. If they got this far, they should already have Awakened... or they're on the brink of doing the opposite.
I mean, why not write something at least? It'll be like a fun little Easter egg.
They might read it and gain the strength they need to overcome a great tragedy.
Frankly, that notion is almost too silly to entertain. Nevertheless... I'll put my faith in that genius instinct of yours, Anzeel.
You should be the first to leave a message though, not me.
Oof... Dumping the toughest task on me, huh?
"Heya, little shipgirl! Good job finding this message! Hope you have a great day!"
I hate to be a critic, but maybe you could... tone it down a little?
Yeah, I guess you're right... Okay, how about this:
"Hang in there, child of the Creator."
"Your very existence is a miracle born from the hopes and hearts of countless people."
"Face the darkness, but don't let it consume you! Mama's cheering for you!"
Better?
Serious, yet light-hearted at the same time... I'm not sure what kind of message that'll send.
Look, I'm a researcher, not a writer! This is the best I can do!
Now it's your turn to write something!
I'm not sure I could come up with something in the spur of the moment... Can I sleep on it first?
Nuh-uh, you're not getting off that easy! You're doing it right now!

options.content: "For those you love, and those who love you, don't give up."
"Chin up and keep fighting. Everyone back home needs you."
"I too would like to meet you, perhaps in another time or place."
"If you make it back to port, drinks are on me."
"ERROR CODE 96421. PLEASE RESTART THE APPLICATION."
Write nothing

Abyssal Refrain.say: Fine... Let's see then––
Don't give up...? What have I given up on?
Home...? I still have a home to return to?
Meet me? Excuse me? Is this a pick-up line?
Famous last words to tell a comrade on the battlefield...
How do I restart it? And what application even is this?
Sigh... Out of all the things you could say, you chose nothing?
These people are... interesting, even if I still have no clue what anyone's talking about.
I recognize one of them. She's the female researcher from before, I think.
And the other person... Nope, doesn't ring a bell. Must be someone important, though.
Mentioned something about a "genius instinct," though... That reminds me of how Soobrazitelny always goes around calling herself a genius.
And then there's Kiev, Volga, and of course, Arkhangelsk...
My... My comrades...!
Yes! I remember now! I remember all of it!
I would never forget about my comrades! And they would never forget about me!
What am I doing here... I've got my duty to fulfill, and the Northern Parliament to return home to!
I know you can hear me! Get me out of here and put me back where I belong!
Take me back home, to where I belong!
FATAL ERROR.
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 99.7%
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 83.5%
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 65.9%
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 41.6%
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 10.2%
ORIGINATION PROGRESS: 0.0%
Kronshtadt's fractured senses pieced themselves back together, her consciousness clawing its way out of the void.
Next came her humanity, then her emotions, flooding back into her being.
*cough* *cough*! Wh-which loop is this?!
Enough is enough, please... I think I've endured enough punishment already...
Kronshtadt! You came back to us!
You wouldn't believe how long you slept. Frankly, I considered waking you with brute force more than a few times.
That explains why my whole body hurts... Violence is never the answer, Arkhangelsk.
I only said I considered it. Maybe I'll give you a friendly whupping after we've returned to reality, though.
Heheh. Hit me with your best shot.
So, what now? Everyone's gathered in one place – do you have a plan?
Yes. I've gathered all the intel we'll need. All that's left is to finish this just the way I like it.
Please pace yourself, okay? We'll gladly take part of the burden off your back.
Exactly! You have a genius mechanic at your disposal – use her!
All we have to do is beat this big thing up, right?
No, we're not doing that. You can't beat a force of nature.
Then what? If we don't strike now while it's sitting around, it'll overwhelm us with its waves again!
The black tornado was still spinning, but its intimidating presence and air of madness were gone. Now it was no more than a destructive storm.
(It's lost its menacing edge... You've shown me everything you had to show, is that it?)
(Or is this just the aftermath of it all, and it's our job to take care of it?)
(If so, it's a humiliating reminder of how stupid I was to think I could fight a tornado with cannon fire.)
It doesn't matter. I will finish this mission no matter what and go home with my comrades and the data we gathered!
And if anything or anyone tries to stand in our way, we will fight until it is no more!
All ships! Aim for the tornado's center! Hit it with everything you've got!
"Prototype submarine Suliko approaching maximum depth."
The Suliko hit the seafloor, sending out shockwaves that rippled through the meteorite and the surrounding environment.
Seemingly in response, the Sirens in the area adopted more aggressive behavioral protocols.
The raised threat level posed a significant challenge for search parties in the area. This, coupled with the confidential nature of the mission, put a temporary stop to major rescue efforts.
But, at no point did any shipgirl from the Northern Parliament forget that their comrades were out here, somewhere.
Their search continued for any lead that might lead them to the girls' whereabouts. They waited, restlessly, for a chance to rescue their missing comrades.
Beneath the endless ice, an echo continued to emanate from the depths, an abyssal refrain waiting to be heard.

Return.say: Northern Parliament Research Base - Nearby Waters
The Northern Parliament's submarine followed the signal into the depths of the ocean.
The time had finally come. With the Commander's aid, the Siren activity in the Polar region was finally curbed enough to allow the search to continue.
Belorussiya, there are some Siren submarines up ahead.
Take them out, Tashkent. Don't miss.
3... 2... 1... Direct hit confirmed. The target is down.
Heh... Good thing we didn't come here defenseless. The data Commander provided for us was invaluable.
This vessel is a more polished product than the Suliko.
But, the Suliko still should have enough energy and supplies to keep those girls alive...
It's hard to say. While there should've been enough supplies to last for a year, no ship should be able to continuously operate in these conditions...
At this point, we can only pray for their safety...
Look, there it is! Kronshtadt's submarine!
Prepare to approach the Suliko, Belorussiya! Let us go greet the heroes of the Northern Parliament!

Survival.say: Back at the Northern Parliament
Sovetsky Soyuz reunited with Kronshtadt at a grand parade celebrating the return of the long-lost heroes.

The fact that they were able to return home safely brought relief to the hearts of many, but just as importantly– the information they gathered while connecting with the meteorite would surely pave a new path for the Northern Parliament.
Some things never change, huh? It's good to be back home.
Indeed. This is the place where we belong, and it will always await us with open arms.
How was your trip back?
I guess I got the first-class treatment? In any case, I slept through most of it.
Somehow, our resident genius mechanic still had the energy to go back to the research base.
Honestly, I never thought I'd be able to see my home again.
Yes, it was a miracle for us as well to find that you were still alive. We've never stopped thinking about your disappearance since that time–
So, time didn't seem to be flowing normally inside the chamber?
Correct. Apparently you stayed asleep inside the chamber the entire time until you were found. But nothing else had changed.
It was as if time stood still.
...Let's set that aside for now. No matter how deep the submarine dove, there's no way that time itself would freeze over inside the chamber.
But, if that had anything to do with the Siren technology we were using, it becomes clear why they are so keenly interested in the meteorite.
...So what do you plan on doing next? Do you want some time off, or do you plan to resume your training?
I am always prepared to give more for the Northern Parliament, Soyuz.
We appreciate your service, comrade.
Welcome home, Kronshtadt.
*sigh*... Hah, it's good to be back, my friend.
...Sorry to have kept you waiting!

Lost Child.say: Royal Isles - Queen's Palace
Suffolk was running through the palace's corridors, a stack of documents in her arms.
She had set the alarm to wake herself up earlier, but slept right through it after having slapped the snooze button repeatedly.
Thus, she had no choice but to try to take some shortcuts through some unfamiliar halls.
Ugh... I'm going to make a fool of myself in front of Her Majesty...
*pant* *pant* ...Huh? Did I make a wrong turn somewhere? This was supposed to get me there faster...
W-wait, did I get lost?!
She noticed that she was no longer surrounded by the palace's brightly-decorated corridors, but instead had entered a strange place with unnatural-looking walls.
She had never seen any place like this before, but assumed it had to be somewhere beneath the palace.
Ugh... Did I somehow end up getting lost in the palace basement?
If the Head Maid finds out about this, she's going to give me an earful...
Oh my, if it isn't Suffolk. I wasn't expecting guests today~
Eep?!
Wh-who's there?!
There's no need to be alarmed, heehee~ I was just thinking how rare it is to see a member of the Maid Team get lost~♪
Miss Neptune! What are you doing here?
Wait, I need to deliver these materials to Her Majesty right away! S-sorry!
I see, I see~ You're on some important business, aren't you?
That being the case, allow me to show you the way~♪
Really? Thank you so much, Miss Neptune! You're a lifesaver!
Phew... Maybe I can avoid sitting through the Head Maid's lectures at this rate...
